publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Includes correspondence, publications,photographs, and newspaper clippings concerning his work at the New York State Agricultural Experiment Station, the Boston Floating Hospital for Infants and Children, the Kellogg and Postum Cereal Companies, and his development of Similac Infant Formula.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Alfred Bosworth was an assistant and associate chemist at the New York State Agricultural Experiment Station at Geneva between 1905-1916. While there he studied the chemical and biological processes involved in the ripening of cheese and conducted studies on the composition of several types of milk, including human milk. During this time Bosworth developed an interest in infant feeding which paved the way for the discovery of Similac Infant Formula.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, publications, newspaper clippings concerning his work at the New York State Agricultural Experiment Station, the Boston Floating Hospital for Infants and Children, the Kellogg and Postum Cereal Companies, and his development of Similac Infant Formula.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Alfred Bosworth papers, #22-2-3998. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

Bowditch and Crown, Cork and Seal Company
Correspondence from People Requesting Bosworth's Infant Formula

Mellin's Food Company; Massachusetts Commission on Mental Diseases (includes proposals)
New York State Agricultural Experiment Station, Geneva, New York
Washington Agricultural Experiment Station; George Washington Coffee Refining Company

Nucoa Butter Company and Abbott Labs
Boston Floating Hospital-Annual Reports (1918, 1921); Reprints (1921-1934); By-laws

Milk Powder-American Dry Milk Institute-First Annual Meeting
Milk Powder-Paper given at 4th Annual American Dry Milk Institute

Lab Reports of People Given Reconstructed Milk
Sketch of the Life and Work of Alfred Bosworth-Vol. V
Reconstructed Milk and the Chemical Investigations Involved with its Development, Lecture-Vol. VI
Bosworth's Reconstructed Milk, Franklin Infant Food, Similac-Vol. VII
Sketch of the Life and Work of Alfred Bosworth-Vol. VIII
Chemistry of Milk in its Relation to Infant Feeding-Vol. X
"Outline for a Process for Making Improved Malted Grape Nuts"
"Value of Dairy Products in the Diet"
"Visit with a Silent Milkman"- by Bosworth
Publications, notebook, John Norton Labs Milk Machinery Plans
Two bound copies of Bosworth's publications
Bank statements, salary agreements, investment options
